title: Modiolastrum malvifolium
intro: Modiolastrum malvifolium, also known as Malvastrum malvifolium, is a plant species belonging to the Malvaceae family, commonly referred to as a type of mallow or a wild hibiscus. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may help alleviate symptoms of various ailments such as digestive issues and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Modiolastrum malvifolium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Modiolastrum malvifolium is classified within the family Malvaceae, commonly known as the mallow or hibiscus family. This family consists of approximately 243 genera and 1,900 species of flowering plants. Modiolastrum malvifolium is a member of the Malvoideae subfamily, which is one of the largest subfamilies within Malvaceae. The Malvoideae subfamily includes genera such as Malva, Sida, and Hibiscus. Modiolastrum malvifolium is a unique species within this subfamily, characterized by its distinct leaf shape and flower morphology.
